fsm_model.section_ref: Matter Core Specification Section 14.4, Cluster ID 0x0801
fsm_model.description: This FSM models the complete lifecycle of TLS Root Certificates and TLS Client Certificates, including provisioning, rotation, lookup, and removal operations. The cluster enforces fabric isolation, resource limits, and referential integrity with TLS Client Management Cluster (0x0802).

states.name: RootCert_NotProvisioned
states.description: No root certificate exists for this CAID. This is the initial state before any certificate provisioning.

states.name: RootCert_Provisioned_NotReferenced
states.description: Root certificate is provisioned and stored but not yet referenced by any TLS endpoint. Can be safely removed or rotated.

states.name: RootCert_Provisioned_Referenced
states.description: Root certificate is in use by at least one TLS endpoint in TLS Client Management Cluster. Cannot be removed (only rotated) until all references are removed.

states.name: ClientCert_NotProvisioned
states.description: No client certificate exists for this CCDID. No key pair generated. Initial state before ClientCSR command.

states.name: ClientCert_KeyGenerated_CertPending
states.description: TLS key pair generated via ClientCSR, CSR returned to client. Awaiting ProvisionClientCertificate to complete the workflow. ClientCertificate field is NULL.

states.name: ClientCert_Provisioned_NotReferenced
states.description: Full client certificate chain provisioned. Certificate public key verified to match stored private key. Not yet used by any TLS endpoint.

state_variables.intermediate_certificates: list[octstr] (0 to 10 certs, each max 3000 bytes)

states.name: ClientCert_Provisioned_Referenced
states.description: Client certificate in use by at least one TLS endpoint. Cannot be removed until all references cleared. Can be rotated.

transitions.description: Reject root certificate provisioning if time not synchronized
Reject invalid DER-encoded certificate
Reject duplicate certificate with same fingerprint in fabric (applies to both new provision and rotation)
Reject new certificate when fabric limit reached

transitions.guard_condition: CAID == NULL && UTCTime != NULL && is_valid_tls_certificate(Certificate) && !fingerprint_exists_in_fabric(compute_fingerprint(Certificate), accessing_fabric) && count_root_certs(accessing_fabric) < MaxRootCertificates
transitions.description: Successfully provision new root certificate with auto-generated CAID
CAID not found for rotation
Fabric isolation: cannot rotate certificate from different fabric

transitions.guard_condition: CAID != NULL && exists_caid(CAID) && fabric_of_caid(CAID) == accessing_fabric && UTCTime != NULL && is_valid_tls_certificate(Certificate) && !fingerprint_exists_in_fabric(compute_fingerprint(Certificate), accessing_fabric)
transitions.timing_requirement: Updated certificate only used for new TLS connections established after this command completes
transitions.description: Successfully rotate existing root certificate (unreferenced)

transitions.guard_condition: CAID != NULL && exists_caid(CAID) && fabric_of_caid(CAID) == accessing_fabric && UTCTime != NULL && is_valid_tls_certificate(Certificate) && !fingerprint_exists_in_fabric(compute_fingerprint(Certificate), accessing_fabric)
transitions.timing_requirement: Updated certificate only used for new TLS connections established after this command completes
transitions.description: Successfully rotate existing root certificate (referenced by endpoints)
Return all root certificates for accessing fabric
Return specific root certificate by CAID
Return CAID for given certificate fingerprint
Successfully remove unreferenced root certificate
Reject removal of root certificate in use by TLS endpoints
Reject new CSR when fabric client certificate limit reached
Reject CSR if generated key pair collides with existing TLS or operational credential keys
Generate new key pair, create CCDID entry, return CSR and nonce signature
Reuse existing key pair for certificate rotation (CSR pending state)
Reuse existing key pair for certificate rotation (not referenced)
Reuse existing key pair for certificate rotation (referenced by endpoints)
CCDID not found
Fabric isolation: cannot access CCDID from different fabric
CCDID not found for provisioning
Fabric isolation: cannot provision certificate for different fabric
Duplicate client certificate fingerprint in fabric
Invalid client certificate or intermediate certificate format
Client certificate public key does not match stored private key
Successfully provision client certificate and intermediate chain

transitions.timing_requirement: Updated certificate only used for new TLS connections established after this command completes
transitions.description: Rotate client certificate (not referenced)

transitions.timing_requirement: Updated certificate only used for new TLS connections established after this command completes
transitions.description: Rotate client certificate (referenced by endpoints)
Remove client certificate entry with pending CSR and key pair
Remove provisioned but unreferenced client certificate and key pair
Reject removal of client certificate in use by TLS endpoints
Return all client certificates for accessing fabric
Return specific client certificate by CCDID
Return CCDID for given client certificate fingerprint

functions.name: crypto_generate_keypair
functions.description: Generate a new TLS key pair using ECDSA. The specification references Crypto_GenerateKeypair without specifying curve, but Matter typically uses P-256 (secp256r1) for ECDSA operations.
functions.algorithm_detail: ECDSA key generation on P-256 curve (secp256r1). Uses DRBG for random number generation. Returns keypair structure containing both public and private keys. Must check for collisions with existing TLS key pairs and operational credential key pairs after generation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR command when CCDID==NULL to generate new key pair for new client certificate. Transition T_ClientCert_002 and T_ClientCert_003.

functions.name: crypto_sign

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Data to be signed (in ClientCSR, this is the Nonce field)

functions.description: Generate ECDSA signature of message using provided private key. This proves possession of the private key corresponding to the public key in the CSR.
functions.algorithm_detail: ECDSA signature generation using SHA-256 hash. Spec references Crypto_Sign(). Input: message (32-byte nonce), privateKey (ECDSA private key from TLS key pair). Output: DER-encoded ECDSA signature (typically 64-72 bytes for P-256). Signature format follows RFC 3279 or SEC1.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR command to sign the nonce field, proving possession of private key. Used in transitions T_ClientCert_003, T_ClientCert_004, T_ClientCert_005, T_ClientCert_006 to generate NonceSignature field.

functions.name: generate_pkcs10_csr

parameters.name: keypair
parameters.description: TLS key pair containing public and private keys

functions.description: Generate a Certificate Signing Request in PKCS#10 format (RFC 2986). CSR contains public key and is signed with private key to prove possession.
functions.algorithm_detail: PKCS#10 CSR generation per RFC 2986 section 4.2. CertificationRequestInfo contains: version=0, subject (MAY be any value, CA may override), subjectPKInfo (contains TLS public key), attributes (optional). Signature algorithm: ECDSA with SHA-256. The CSR includes signature using private key over the encoded CertificationRequestInfo. Output: DER-encoded PKCS#10 CSR.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR command to generate CSR for certificate authority. Used in transitions T_ClientCert_003, T_ClientCert_004, T_ClientCert_005, T_ClientCert_006 to populate CSR field in response.

functions.name: compute_fingerprint
functions.description: Compute cryptographic hash of certificate to create unique fingerprint for duplicate detection and lookup operations.
functions.algorithm_detail: Certificate fingerprint computation, likely SHA-256 hash of DER-encoded certificate bytes (32 bytes output). Specification allows 'max 64 bytes' suggesting SHA-512 is also possible. Hash function must be collision-resistant. Used for: (1) duplicate detection in ProvisionRootCertificate/ProvisionClientCertificate, (2) lookup operations via LookupRootCertificate/LookupClientCertificate.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when provisioning certificates to compute fingerprint for storage and duplicate checking. Also used in guard conditions checking fingerprint_exists_in_fabric().

functions.name: is_valid_tls_certificate
functions.description: Validate that certificate is properly DER-encoded and conforms to TLS certificate format (X.509v3).
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse DER encoding of X.509 certificate. Check: (1) Valid DER structure (no truncation, correct ASN.1 encoding), (2) Valid X.509 certificate fields (version, serialNumber, signature, issuer, validity, subject, subjectPublicKeyInfo, etc.), (3) Supported signature algorithm, (4) Valid public key format. Does NOT check certificate chain or expiration, only structural validity.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionRootCertificate and ProvisionClientCertificate guard conditions to validate certificate format before storage.

functions.name: is_invalid_tls_certificate
functions.description: Negation of is_valid_tls_certificate. Returns true if certificate is invalid.
functions.algorithm_detail: Returns !is_valid_tls_certificate(certificate)
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions to check for invalid certificates

functions.name: any_invalid_cert_in_list
functions.description: Check if any certificate in the list is invalid. Returns true if at least one invalid certificate found.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through cert_list, apply is_invalid_tls_certificate() to each entry. Return true on first invalid certificate, false if all valid.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionClientCertificate guard conditions to validate IntermediateCertificates list

functions.name: all_valid_certs_in_list
functions.description: Check if all certificates in list are valid. Returns true only if all certificates pass validation.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through cert_list, apply is_valid_tls_certificate() to each entry. Return false on first invalid certificate, true if all valid or list empty.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionClientCertificate guard conditions to validate IntermediateCertificates list

functions.name: public_private_key_correspondence
functions.description: Verify that the public key in the certificate mathematically corresponds to the provided private key.
functions.algorithm_detail: Extract subjectPublicKeyInfo from certificate (DER-decode X.509). For ECDSA: Verify that private_key * G == public_key (where G is generator point on curve). Alternatively, sign test message with private_key, verify signature with public_key from certificate. Returns true if correspondence confirmed, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Critical security check in ProvisionClientCertificate to ensure certificate issued matches the CSR generated with stored key pair. Prevents certificate substitution attacks.

functions.name: key_collision_detected_after_generation
functions.description: Check if newly generated key pair collides with any existing TLS key pair or operational credential key pair on the node.
functions.algorithm_detail: After crypto_generate_keypair(), compare new keypair against: (1) All existing TLS private keys in ProvisionedClientCertificates across all fabrics, (2) All operational credential private keys (from Node Operational Credentials cluster). For ECDSA keys, compare private key scalar values. If match found, return true (collision detected). Spec notes: 'Barring software error' this should be extremely rare.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR after key generation to detect collisions. If collision detected, discard key pair and fail with DYNAMIC_CONSTRAINT_ERROR.

functions.name: generate_unique_tlscaid
functions.description: Generate a unique TLSCAID value that does not collide with any existing CAID in ProvisionedRootCertificates list across all fabrics.
functions.algorithm_detail: Spec guidance: Start at 0, monotonically increment by 1 for each new allocation. When reaching 65534, wrap to 0. Check for collision with all existing CAIDs (across all fabrics on node). If collision found, increment and retry until unique value found. Returns unique CAID value. Note: Global uniqueness across fabrics, not per-fabric.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionRootCertificate when CAID==NULL to assign new unique identifier for root certificate entry.

functions.name: generate_unique_tlsccdid
functions.description: Generate a unique TLSCCDID value that does not collide with any existing CCDID in ProvisionedClientCertificates list across all fabrics.
functions.algorithm_detail: Spec guidance: Start at 0, monotonically increment by 1 for each new allocation. When reaching 65534, wrap to 0. Check for collision with all existing CCDIDs (across all fabrics on node). If collision found, increment and retry until unique value found. Returns unique CCDID value. Note: Global uniqueness across fabrics, not per-fabric.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR when CCDID==NULL to assign new unique identifier for client certificate entry.

functions.name: exists_caid
functions.description: Check if CAID exists in ProvisionedRootCertificates list (any fabric).
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through ProvisionedRootCertificates list. Return true if any entry has CAID field matching input caid. Return false if no match found.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions for ProvisionRootCertificate (rotation), FindRootCertificate, LookupRootCertificate, RemoveRootCertificate

functions.name: exists_ccdid

parameters.name: ccdid
parameters.description: Client certificate details ID to check

functions.description: Check if CCDID exists in ProvisionedClientCertificates list (any fabric).
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through ProvisionedClientCertificates list. Return true if any entry has CCDID field matching input ccdid. Return false if no match found.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions for ClientCSR (rotation), ProvisionClientCertificate, FindClientCertificate, LookupClientCertificate, RemoveClientCertificate

functions.name: fabric_of_caid
functions.description: Return the associated fabric for given CAID.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup CAID in ProvisionedRootCertificates list. Return the associated_fabric value from TLSCertStruct entry. Assumes exists_caid(caid)==true (should be checked first).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions to enforce fabric isolation: verify accessing_fabric == fabric_of_caid(CAID)

functions.name: fabric_of_ccdid
functions.description: Return the associated fabric for given CCDID.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup CCDID in ProvisionedClientCertificates list. Return the associated_fabric value from TLSClientCertificateDetailStruct entry. Assumes exists_ccdid(ccdid)==true (should be checked first).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions to enforce fabric isolation: verify accessing_fabric == fabric_of_ccdid(CCDID)

functions.name: referenced_by_endpoint
functions.description: Check if certificate ID is referenced by any TLS endpoint in TLS Client Management Cluster (0x0802) ProvisionedEndpoints list.
functions.algorithm_detail: Query TLS Client Management Cluster (cluster ID 0x0802). Check ProvisionedEndpoints list for any entry where CAID field (for root certs) or CCDID field (for client certs) matches input id. Return true if referenced, false otherwise. This is cross-cluster dependency check.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Critical security check in RemoveRootCertificate and RemoveClientCertificate. Prevents removal of certificates still in use by active TLS endpoints. Spec mandates INVALID_IN_STATE if referenced.

functions.name: fingerprint_exists_in_fabric
functions.description: Check if given fingerprint already exists for any certificate in the specified fabric.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through ProvisionedRootCertificates and ProvisionedClientCertificates lists. For entries where associated_fabric==fabric, compare computed fingerprints with input fingerprint. Return true if match found, false otherwise. Used to prevent duplicate certificates within a fabric.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in ProvisionRootCertificate and ProvisionClientCertificate guard conditions to reject ALREADY_EXISTS duplicates

functions.name: exists_fingerprint_in_fabric
functions.description: Alias for fingerprint_exists_in_fabric, used in Lookup commands.
functions.algorithm_detail: Same as fingerprint_exists_in_fabric
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in LookupRootCertificate and LookupClientCertificate guard conditions

functions.name: get_caid_by_fingerprint
functions.description: Return the CAID for certificate with given fingerprint in specified fabric.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through ProvisionedRootCertificates. Find entry where associated_fabric==fabric AND computed_fingerprint==fingerprint. Return CAID field from that entry. Assumes exists_fingerprint_in_fabric()==true (should be checked first).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in LookupRootCertificate transition to return CAID in response

functions.name: get_ccdid_by_fingerprint
functions.description: Return the CCDID for client certificate with given fingerprint in specified fabric.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through ProvisionedClientCertificates. Find entry where associated_fabric==fabric AND computed_fingerprint==fingerprint. Return CCDID field from that entry. Assumes exists_fingerprint_in_fabric()==true (should be checked first).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in LookupClientCertificate transition to return CCDID in response

functions.name: count_root_certs
functions.description: Count number of root certificates provisioned for given fabric.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through ProvisionedRootCertificates list. Count entries where associated_fabric==fabric. Return count.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in ProvisionRootCertificate guard condition to check against MaxRootCertificates limit before allowing new provision

functions.name: count_client_certs
functions.description: Count number of client certificates provisioned for given fabric.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through ProvisionedClientCertificates list. Count entries where associated_fabric==fabric. Return count.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in ClientCSR guard condition to check against MaxClientCertificates limit before allowing new key generation

functions.name: create_tlscertstruct
functions.description: Create new TLSCertStruct with provided values.
functions.algorithm_detail: Allocate new TLSCertStruct. Set CAID field=caid, Certificate field=certificate, computed fingerprint=fingerprint. Associate with fabric (fabric-scoped). Return struct.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionRootCertificate when provisioning new certificate (CAID==NULL)

functions.name: add_to_provisioned_root_certificates
functions.description: Add TLSCertStruct to ProvisionedRootCertificates list attribute.
functions.algorithm_detail: Append struct to ProvisionedRootCertificates list. List constraint: max MaxRootCertificates per fabric. Fabric scoping handled automatically.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionRootCertificate transition T_RootCert_005 after creating new struct

functions.name: update_certificate_field
functions.description: Update the Certificate field of existing TLSCertStruct entry for certificate rotation.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup entry in ProvisionedRootCertificates with matching CAID. Update Certificate field with new certificate value. Note: Spec states updated certificate only used for NEW TLS connections established after this call.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionRootCertificate rotation transitions T_RootCert_008, T_RootCert_009

functions.name: update_fingerprint_field
functions.description: Update the fingerprint for existing root certificate entry after rotation.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup entry in ProvisionedRootCertificates with matching CAID. Update stored fingerprint with new value.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionRootCertificate rotation transitions after updating certificate

functions.name: remove_root_cert_entry
functions.description: Remove TLSCertStruct entry from ProvisionedRootCertificates list.
functions.algorithm_detail: Find entry in ProvisionedRootCertificates with matching CAID. Remove from list. Free memory.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in RemoveRootCertificate transition T_RootCert_013

functions.name: create_tlsclientcertdetailstruct
functions.description: Create new TLSClientCertificateDetailStruct with CCDID, initially NULL certificate fields.
functions.algorithm_detail: Allocate new TLSClientCertificateDetailStruct. Set CCDID field=ccdid. Set ClientCertificate=NULL, IntermediateCertificates=NULL. Associate with fabric (fabric-scoped). Return struct.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR when generating new key pair (CCDID==NULL)

functions.name: associate_keypair
functions.description: Associate generated key pair with CCDID entry in secure storage.
functions.algorithm_detail: Store keypair (both public and private keys) in secure key storage, indexed by CCDID. Private key must be protected from unauthorized access. Implement secure memory handling.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR transition T_ClientCert_003 after key generation

functions.name: set_client_certificate_null
functions.description: Set ClientCertificate field to NULL for CCDID entry.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup entry in ProvisionedClientCertificates with matching CCDID. Set ClientCertificate field to NULL. This indicates CSR generated but ProvisionClientCertificate not yet called.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR transition T_ClientCert_003 when creating new entry

functions.name: set_intermediate_certificates_null
functions.description: Set IntermediateCertificates field to NULL or empty list for CCDID entry.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup entry in ProvisionedClientCertificates with matching CCDID. Set IntermediateCertificates field to NULL or empty list. This indicates CSR generated but certificate chain not yet provisioned.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR transition T_ClientCert_003 when creating new entry

functions.name: add_to_provisioned_client_certificates
functions.description: Add TLSClientCertificateDetailStruct to ProvisionedClientCertificates list attribute.
functions.algorithm_detail: Append struct to ProvisionedClientCertificates list. List constraint: max MaxClientCertificates per fabric. Fabric scoping handled automatically.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR transition T_ClientCert_003 after creating new struct

functions.name: update_client_certificate_field
functions.description: Update ClientCertificate field of existing CCDID entry.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup entry in ProvisionedClientCertificates with matching CCDID. Update ClientCertificate field with certificate value. Changes NULL to non-NULL on first provision. Updates existing value on rotation. Note: Spec states updated certificate only used for NEW TLS connections.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionClientCertificate transitions T_ClientCert_014, T_ClientCert_015, T_ClientCert_016

functions.name: update_intermediate_certificates_field
functions.description: Update IntermediateCertificates field of existing CCDID entry.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup entry in ProvisionedClientCertificates with matching CCDID. Update IntermediateCertificates field with cert_list. Can be empty list (direct signing by root CA). Max 10 intermediate certificates, each max 3000 bytes.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionClientCertificate transitions T_ClientCert_014, T_ClientCert_015, T_ClientCert_016

functions.name: update_fingerprint_field_client
functions.description: Update fingerprint for client certificate entry.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup entry in ProvisionedClientCertificates with matching CCDID. Update stored fingerprint with computed value from client certificate.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionClientCertificate transitions after updating certificate

functions.name: remove_client_cert_entry

parameters.name: ccdid
parameters.description: Client certificate details ID to remove

functions.description: Remove TLSClientCertificateDetailStruct entry from ProvisionedClientCertificates list.
functions.algorithm_detail: Find entry in ProvisionedClientCertificates with matching CCDID. Remove from list. Free memory allocated for struct (but not key pair - that's handled separately).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in RemoveClientCertificate transitions T_ClientCert_017, T_ClientCert_018

functions.name: remove_tls_keypair
functions.description: Securely delete TLS key pair associated with CCDID. CRITICAL: Must securely erase private key from memory.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup key pair in secure key storage by CCDID. Securely erase private key from memory (overwrite with zeros or random data to prevent recovery). Delete public key. Remove key storage entry. Spec requirement: 'Remove the TLS Key Pair belonging to the passed in CCDID.' This is critical for security - orphaned private keys enable impersonation attacks.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in RemoveClientCertificate transitions T_ClientCert_017, T_ClientCert_018. Security property: private key cleanup prevents post-deletion key recovery attacks.

functions.name: get_keypair
functions.description: Retrieve TLS key pair associated with CCDID from secure storage.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup key pair in secure key storage by CCDID. Return keypair structure containing both public and private keys. Used for CSR generation during rotation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR transitions when CCDID!=NULL (rotation) to reuse existing key pair

functions.name: get_private_key
functions.description: Retrieve TLS private key associated with CCDID from secure storage.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup key pair in secure key storage by CCDID. Extract and return private key component only.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionClientCertificate guard conditions to verify public-private key correspondence

functions.name: get_private_key_from_keypair
functions.description: Extract private key from keypair structure.
functions.algorithm_detail: Access private key field from keypair structure and return it.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR transitions to extract private key for signing operations

functions.name: get_all_root_certs_for_fabric
functions.description: Return list of all root certificates for specified fabric.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through ProvisionedRootCertificates. Collect all entries where associated_fabric==fabric. Return list of TLSCertStruct. When read over Large Message capable transport, include Certificate field. When read over non-Large Message transport, exclude Certificate field.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in FindRootCertificate transition T_RootCert_010 when CAID==NULL

functions.name: get_root_cert
functions.description: Return specific root certificate by CAID.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup entry in ProvisionedRootCertificates with matching CAID. Return TLSCertStruct. Apply same Large Message transport rules as get_all_root_certs_for_fabric.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in FindRootCertificate transition T_RootCert_011 when CAID!=NULL

functions.name: get_all_client_certs_for_fabric
functions.description: Return list of all client certificates for specified fabric.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through ProvisionedClientCertificates. Collect all entries where associated_fabric==fabric. Return list of TLSClientCertificateDetailStruct. When read over Large Message capable transport, include ClientCertificate and IntermediateCertificates fields. When read over non-Large Message transport, exclude these fields. Note: If ClientCertificate==NULL, CSR generated but provisioning not yet complete.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in FindClientCertificate transition T_ClientCert_020 when CCDID==NULL

functions.name: get_client_cert
functions.description: Return specific client certificate by CCDID.
functions.algorithm_detail: Lookup entry in ProvisionedClientCertificates with matching CCDID. Return TLSClientCertificateDetailStruct. Apply same Large Message transport rules as get_all_client_certs_for_fabric.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in FindClientCertificate transition T_ClientCert_021 when CCDID!=NULL

functions.name: increment_count_root_certs
functions.description: Increment per-fabric root certificate counter.
functions.algorithm_detail: Increment internal counter tracking number of root certificates for fabric. Used to enforce MaxRootCertificates limit.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionRootCertificate transition T_RootCert_005 after adding new certificate

functions.name: decrement_count_root_certs
functions.description: Decrement per-fabric root certificate counter.
functions.algorithm_detail: Decrement internal counter tracking number of root certificates for fabric.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in RemoveRootCertificate transition T_RootCert_013 after removing certificate

functions.name: increment_count_client_certs
functions.description: Increment per-fabric client certificate counter.
functions.algorithm_detail: Increment internal counter tracking number of client certificates for fabric. Used to enforce MaxClientCertificates limit.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR transition T_ClientCert_003 after adding new certificate entry

functions.name: decrement_count_client_certs
functions.description: Decrement per-fabric client certificate counter.
functions.algorithm_detail: Decrement internal counter tracking number of client certificates for fabric.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in RemoveClientCertificate transitions T_ClientCert_017, T_ClientCert_018 after removing certificate

functions.name: discard_keypair
functions.description: Securely discard generated key pair after collision detection.
functions.algorithm_detail: Securely erase private key from memory (overwrite with zeros). Delete keypair structure. Called only when key collision detected.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR transition T_ClientCert_002 when key collision detected

functions.name: return_provision_root_certificate_response
functions.description: Return ProvisionRootCertificateResponse command with CAID field.
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct ProvisionRootCertificateResponse (command ID 0x01) with CAID field populated. Send to client.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProvisionRootCertificate success transitions

functions.name: return_client_csr_response
functions.description: Return ClientCSRResponse command with CCDID, CSR, and NonceSignature fields.
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct ClientCSRResponse (command ID 0x08) with CCDID, CSR (max 3000 bytes), and NonceSignature (max 128 bytes) fields. Send to client. Client uses CSR to request certificate from CA, then calls ProvisionClientCertificate with issued certificate.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ClientCSR success transitions

functions.name: return_find_root_certificate_response
functions.description: Return FindRootCertificateResponse command with certificate list.
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct FindRootCertificateResponse (command ID 0x03) with CertificateDetails field containing cert_list. Minimum 1 entry. Send to client.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in FindRootCertificate transitions

functions.name: return_lookup_root_certificate_response
functions.description: Return LookupRootCertificateResponse command with CAID field.
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct LookupRootCertificateResponse (command ID 0x05) with CAID field. Send to client.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in LookupRootCertificate transitions

functions.name: return_find_client_certificate_response

parameters.name: cert_list
parameters.description: List of client certificates (1 to MaxClientCertificates)

functions.description: Return FindClientCertificateResponse command with certificate list.
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct FindClientCertificateResponse (command ID 0x0B) with CertificateDetails field containing cert_list. Minimum 1 entry. Send to client.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in FindClientCertificate transitions

functions.name: return_lookup_client_certificate_response
functions.description: Return LookupClientCertificateResponse command with CCDID field.
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct LookupClientCertificateResponse (command ID 0x0D) with CCDID field. Send to client.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in LookupClientCertificate transitions

functions.name: return_status

parameters.name: status_code
parameters.description: Status code: SUCCESS, NOT_FOUND, INVALID_IN_STATE, ALREADY_EXISTS, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, DYNAMIC_CONSTRAINT_ERROR

functions.description: Return status response for commands with 'Y' response type (generic status).
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct status response with specified status code. Send to client. Used by commands that don't have specific response command defined (RemoveRootCertificate, ProvisionClientCertificate, RemoveClientCertificate).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in various error and success transitions

definitions.definition: Data structures and commands are isolated per fabric. A fabric represents a separate administrative domain with its own certificates, keys, and access control. Cross-fabric access is forbidden - nodes can only access resources associated with their current accessing fabric.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical for multi-tenant security. Fabric isolation prevents one fabric from accessing, modifying, or removing another fabric's certificates and keys. Violation enables cross-fabric privilege escalation and impersonation attacks.

definitions.definition: The fabric identity of the principal (node) executing the command. All commands enforce that accessing_fabric matches the associated_fabric of resources being accessed.
definitions.security_relevance: Basis for access control enforcement. Specification uses 'accessing fabric' throughout to enforce fabric isolation without defining authentication mechanism. Assumes fabric identity is authenticated at transport layer.

definitions.definition: Commands in this cluster uniformly use the Large Message qualifier (marked 'L' in Quality column) to support certificate data up to 3000 bytes. Commands work over both Large Message capable and non-Large Message capable transports, with conditional field inclusion.
definitions.security_relevance: Low security impact. Affects protocol efficiency and field inclusion rules. Non-Large Message transports exclude large fields (Certificate, ClientCertificate, IntermediateCertificates) to prevent message truncation.

definitions.definition: Certificate (root or client) is in use by at least one TLS endpoint in TLS Client Management Cluster (0x0802). Reference tracked in ProvisionedEndpoints list by CAID or CCDID.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical for referential integrity. Prevents removal of certificates while TLS endpoints actively use them. Violation causes TLS connection failures and potential service disruption. Spec mandates INVALID_IN_STATE error for removal attempts.

definitions.definition: Timestamp attribute from Time Synchronization cluster. Must be non-NULL before provisioning root certificates. Null indicates time not yet synchronized.
definitions.security_relevance: Required for certificate validity checking. Provisioning certificates before time sync allows expired certificates to be installed, bypassing temporal security controls. Spec mandates INVALID_IN_STATE check.

definitions.definition: Distinguished Encoding Rules - canonical binary encoding for X.509 certificates. All certificates in this cluster use DER encoding (not PEM or other formats).
definitions.security_relevance: Ensures consistent parsing and fingerprint computation. Invalid DER encoding can trigger parser vulnerabilities. Spec mandates validation before storage.

definitions.definition: Public Key Cryptography Standards #10 Certificate Signing Request format (RFC 2986). Contains public key, subject DN (optional), and signature proving possession of private key.
definitions.security_relevance: CSR signature proves key possession, preventing CSR substitution attacks. Spec mandates additional NonceSignature to further prove possession independent of CSR format.

definitions.definition: Intermediate Certificate Authority Certificate - certificates in chain between client certificate and root CA. Maximum 10 ICACs supported, each max 3000 bytes.
definitions.security_relevance: Forms certificate chain for trust validation. Invalid or excessive ICACs can break TLS handshake or enable denial of service via deep chain validation.

definitions.definition: Updating an existing certificate (root or client) by providing non-NULL CAID/CCDID with new certificate value. Rotated certificate only affects new TLS connections established after rotation completes.
definitions.security_relevance: Allows certificate renewal without service disruption. Active connections continue using old certificate. Critical: rotation does not force disconnection, creating window where both old and new certificates are valid.

definitions.definition: Newly generated TLS key pair matches an existing TLS key pair or operational credential key pair. Must be detected and rejected.
definitions.security_relevance: Key collision enables impersonation - two entities share same private key, breaking authentication and non-repudiation. Spec notes 'barring software error' this should be extremely rare, suggesting reliance on DRBG quality.

definitions.definition: Commands marked with 'A' access modifier require administrator-level privilege. Includes all mutation operations: ProvisionRootCertificate, RemoveRootCertificate, ClientCSR, ProvisionClientCertificate, RemoveClientCertificate.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical access control layer. Unprivileged access to mutations enables certificate injection, removal of trust anchors, and key generation. Compromises entire certificate infrastructure. Enforcement mechanism not defined in spec.

definitions.definition: Cryptographic hash of certificate (max 64 bytes, suggesting SHA-256 or SHA-512). Used for duplicate detection and lookup operations.
definitions.security_relevance: Collision-resistant hash required for security. Weak hash enables duplicate certificates with different content but same fingerprint, bypassing uniqueness checks.

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: ECDSA key generation on P-256 curve (secp256r1)
cryptographic_operations.output: ECDSA keypair (256-bit private key scalar, public key point on P-256)
assumptions: DRBG provides sufficient entropy (256 bits minimum)
Key generation implementation is side-channel resistant
Generated keys are uniformly distributed over key space
Collision probability negligible: 2^-256

cryptographic_operations.output: DER-encoded ECDSA signature (typically 64-72 bytes for P-256)
assumptions: ECDSA implementation uses deterministic nonce (RFC 6979) or secure random nonce
Private key never leaves secure storage during signing
Signature timing is constant to prevent side-channel attacks
CSR signature algorithm is ECDSA-SHA256
Subject DN may be ignored by CA (spec notes)
CSR format conforms to RFC 2986 section 4.2

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: SHA-256 or SHA-512 hash of DER-encoded certificate
cryptographic_operations.output: Hash digest (32 bytes for SHA-256 or 64 bytes for SHA-512)
assumptions: Hash algorithm is collision-resistant
Same certificate always produces same fingerprint (deterministic)
Fingerprint uniqueness within fabric enforced by spec

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: ECDSA signature verification or point multiplication check
assumptions: Public key extracted from certificate subjectPublicKeyInfo
Verification method: sign test message with private key, verify with public key from certificate
Alternative: verify private_key * G == public_key_from_cert (where G is generator point)

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: ASN.1 DER parsing and X.509v3 structure validation
assumptions: Parser is robust against malformed DER (no buffer overflows)
Validation checks: DER structure, X.509 version, signature algorithm support, public key format
Does NOT validate certificate chain or expiration (only structural validity)

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: Comparison of private key scalar values
assumptions: Access to all existing private keys for comparison
Collision probability should be negligible (2^-256 for P-256)
Spec notes 'barring software error' - implies rare occurrence

security_properties.description: All certificate operations enforce fabric isolation. Accessing fabric must match associated fabric of certificate resources. No cross-fabric access permitted.
critical_transitions: All transitions with fabric_of_caid(CAID) != accessing_fabric or fabric_of_ccdid(CCDID) != accessing_fabric guards

security_properties.description: Certificates cannot be removed while referenced by TLS endpoints. INVALID_IN_STATE enforced for removal attempts of referenced certificates.
Per-fabric limits enforced: MaxRootCertificates (min 5), MaxClientCertificates (min 2). RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED returned when limits reached.
No duplicate certificate fingerprints within a fabric. ALREADY_EXISTS returned for duplicates.
Client certificate public key must match stored private key. DYNAMIC_CONSTRAINT_ERROR if mismatch detected.
Newly generated TLS key pairs must not collide with existing TLS or operational credential keys. DYNAMIC_CONSTRAINT_ERROR if collision detected.
Root certificate provisioning requires UTCTime != NULL (time synchronized). INVALID_IN_STATE if UTCTime NULL.
All provisioned certificates must be valid DER-encoded TLS certificates. DYNAMIC_CONSTRAINT_ERROR for invalid format.
When client certificate removed, associated TLS key pair must be securely deleted. Prevents orphaned private key recovery attacks.
Updated certificates (via rotation) only affect new TLS connections established after rotation completes. Active connections continue using old certificate.
ClientCSR returns signature of nonce using TLS private key, proving possession of private key corresponding to CSR public key. Prevents CSR substitution attacks.
All mutation operations (Provision*, Remove*, ClientCSR) require admin privilege (A access modifier). Unprivileged access rejected.
Maximum 10 intermediate certificates allowed in certificate chain, each max 3000 bytes. Empty list permitted for direct root CA signing.

security_assumptions.assumption: Time Synchronization cluster provides accurate, monotonic UTCTime before certificate provisioning
security_assumptions.if_violated: Certificates can be provisioned without valid timestamps, enabling expired certificate attacks and replay vulnerabilities. Certificate validity checking becomes unreliable.

security_assumptions.assumption: TLS Client Management Cluster (0x0802) enforces referential integrity with CAID/CCDID values
security_assumptions.if_violated: Certificates can be removed while endpoints reference them, causing dangling pointers. Active TLS connections fail with authentication errors, enabling denial of service.

security_assumptions.assumption: Fabric identity is authenticated at transport layer and cannot be spoofed
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker spoofs accessing_fabric to bypass fabric isolation. Enables cross-fabric certificate theft, rotation hijacking, and privilege escalation across administrative domains.

security_assumptions.assumption: Admin privilege (A access modifier) is securely granted and cannot be escalated by unprivileged principals
security_assumptions.if_violated: Unprivileged attacker gains mutation access to inject malicious certificates, remove trust anchors, or corrupt certificate storage. Complete compromise of certificate infrastructure.

security_assumptions.assumption: Cryptographic primitives (ECDSA, DER parsing, fingerprint computation) are implemented correctly without vulnerabilities
security_assumptions.if_violated: Malformed certificates bypass validation, invalid signatures accepted, key correspondence check fails. Enables certificate forgery, impersonation, and buffer overflow attacks.

security_assumptions.assumption: DRBG (Deterministic Random Bit Generator) provides sufficient entropy for key generation without predictable outputs
security_assumptions.if_violated: Key collision probability increases dramatically. Attacker predicts key generation to trigger collisions or directly compute private keys, breaking all cryptographic security.

security_assumptions.assumption: Memory used for private key storage is protected against unauthorized read access and securely erased on deletion
security_assumptions.if_violated: Deleted private keys remain in memory or swap. Attacker with memory access reads orphaned keys to impersonate deleted clients or decrypt past TLS sessions.

security_assumptions.assumption: Certificate Authority (external to cluster) validates CSRs properly and issues certificates with correct subjectPublicKey matching CSR
security_assumptions.if_violated: CA issues certificate with wrong public key. ProvisionClientCertificate succeeds but TLS handshake fails due to key mismatch, causing service disruption.

security_assumptions.assumption: Large Message capability detection is reliable; transport correctly reports its capabilities
security_assumptions.if_violated: Certificate data included on limited transport causes truncation; excluded on capable transport forces extra round trips. Protocol errors or denial of service via bandwidth exhaustion.

security_assumptions.assumption: Certificate fingerprint computation (hash algorithm) is collision-resistant and consistently implemented
security_assumptions.if_violated: Fingerprint collisions create ambiguous certificate identification. Attacker finds collision to substitute malicious certificate, bypassing certificate pinning and trust verification.

security_assumptions.assumption: Root node endpoint accessibility is restricted to authorized administrators
security_assumptions.if_violated: Cluster SHALL be present only on root node endpoint. If exposed on other endpoints or to unauthorized principals, attackers gain direct access to certificate management operations.

security_assumptions.assumption: CSR subject DN is not trusted by device; CA overrides subject DN in issued certificate
security_assumptions.if_violated: If device trusts CSR subject DN without CA validation, attacker submits CSR with privileged subject DN to gain unauthorized privileges through certificate issuance.

validation_checklist.note_on_timers: No timers in this cluster. Certificate rotation timing requirement documented in transition timing_requirement field.
validation_checklist.note_on_guards: Guards use ordered evaluation (most restrictive first) and are collectively exhaustive for each command
validation_checklist.note_on_error_states: No dedicated error states. Errors handled via stay-transitions that return error status codes (INVALID_IN_STATE, NOT_FOUND, ALREADY_EXISTS, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, DYNAMIC_CONSTRAINT_ERROR)
validation_checklist.note_on_universal_preconditions: Per specification, certain validation checks apply universally regardless of state: (1) UTCTime != null for ProvisionRootCertificate, (2) is_valid_tls_certificate for all certificate provisioning, (3) fingerprint_exists_in_fabric check for duplicate prevention applies to BOTH new provisioning AND rotation. These checks occur before state-specific logic per spec's 'Effect on Receipt' ordering.
validation_checklist.note_on_spec_compliance: FSM verified against Matter Core Specification 14.4. All 9 commands covered (ProvisionRootCertificate, FindRootCertificate, LookupRootCertificate, RemoveRootCertificate, ClientCSR, ProvisionClientCertificate, FindClientCertificate, LookupClientCertificate, RemoveClientCertificate). All error conditions from spec 'Effect on Receipt' sections captured. Guard conditions updated 2025-02-13 to fix missing fingerprint checks in rotation transitions (T_RootCert_009, T_ClientCert_015, T_ClientCert_016).
